title: TCW Transform 500 ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (VOTE)
summary: The TCW Transform 500 ETF (VOTE) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $80.22 based on analyst ratings for its underlying holdings. The ETF's portfolio includes major companies like NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, with a significant portion of the portfolio composed of companies covered by 861 analyst ratings in the past year. These ratings and price targets are derived by MarketBeat from individual stock ratings within the ETF's holdings.

title: Nvidia’s Next-Generation AI Chip Rollout Slowed by Engineering Snags
summary: Nvidia's rollout of its next-generation AI chips, specifically two advanced models from the Blackwell lineup, has been delayed due to engineering snags. The issues are prompting a rework of a version of the AI accelerator to ensure better compatibility with existing data center infrastructure designed for the earlier Hopper H100 chip. These delays are impacting Nvidia's efforts to extend its market dominance in AI computing.

title: ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) Stock Plummets 16% After Accidental Q3 Earnings Release Reveals Weaker Bookings and Reduced 2025 Sales Forecast
summary: ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) stock plummeted 16% after an accidental early release of its Q3 earnings report revealed weaker-than-expected bookings and a reduced 2025 sales forecast. Despite strong Q3 results, net bookings of €2.6 billion fell short of predictions between €4 billion and €6 billion, with the company citing delays in EUV demand and a more gradual market recovery. This caused investor concern regarding ASML's future growth trajectory, despite overall bullish sentiment in AI-linked stocks.

title: Nvidia resolves Blackwell design flaw with TSMC
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced the resolution of a design flaw in the new Blackwell GPU series, overcoming production challenges with TSMC's assistance. This development is crucial for the generative AI market, where the Blackwell chips are intended for use.

title: Japan Cloud Leaders Build NVIDIA AI Infrastructure to Transform Industries for the Age of AI
summary: NVIDIA announced that several prominent Japanese cloud providers, including SoftBank Corp., GMO Internet Group, Highreso, KDDI, Rutilea, and SAKURA Internet, are adopting NVIDIA's accelerated computing, networking, and software to build AI infrastructure. These initiatives are supported by the Japan Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry (METI) and aim to transform industries such as robotics, automotive, healthcare, and telecommunications for the age of AI. The cloud services will leverage NVIDIA technologies like Blackwell platforms, H200 Tensor Core GPUs, and HGX systems across various regions in Japan.

title: Can I Buy a Bitcoin Spot ETF in The UK? | Morningstar UK
summary: Retail investor interest in Bitcoin has surged following a record high price and the approval of spot Bitcoin ETFs in the U.S. However, UK investors are currently unable to directly buy these U.S. ETFs due to regulatory differences and the FCA's cautious stance on cryptocurrency. The article explores the reasons behind the UK's stricter regulations and alternative ways for investors to gain exposure to Bitcoin.

title: AMD shares drop more than 6%, close at lowest since 2023 on disappointing data center revenue
summary: AMD's shares dropped over 6% after the company's data center business underperformed Wall Street's revenue estimates, despite overall better-than-expected top and bottom-line results. The chipmaker reported data center sales of $3.86 billion, falling short of the $4.14 billion analysts anticipated. Following this, several Wall Street firms, including Citi and JPMorgan, trimmed their price targets on AMD, citing concerns about competition from Nvidia and potential first-half weakness.

title: AI Data Center Demand Isn't Collapsing, It's Shifting
summary: This article argues that reports of Microsoft canceling data center leases do not signify a collapse in AI data center demand but rather a strategic shift in how OpenAI's infrastructure needs are met. Microsoft is offloading some of OpenAI's substantial data center requirements to other partners like SoftBank and Oracle, especially after the "Stargate Project" announcement, while still maintaining its own CapEx targets. This move ensures that the immense capital expenditures for AI infrastructure are conserved and merely redistributed among various partners.

title: Nebius Slides Post-Earnings: A Long-Term Buy Opportunity?
summary: Nebius Group (NASDAQ: NBIS) shares have dropped over 28% from their recent highs following a mixed Q4 2024 earnings report, characterized by surging revenue but continued profitability challenges. Despite analyst disagreement, with some raising price targets and others reversing bullish stances, the company's strong AI infrastructure growth and ambitious 2025 guidance suggest potential for long-term investors. Investors are watching for NVIDIA's upcoming earnings report for broader AI sector insights.

title: Micron forecasts upbeat quarterly revenue on strong AI memory chip demand
summary: Micron Technology projected third-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates, driven by robust demand for its high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips crucial for AI models. The company reported that all its HBM chips are sold out for calendar year 2025, indicating strong market confidence. Micron also expects improved profitability in fiscal 2025 across both data center and consumer markets, despite potential uncertainties from new tariffs.

title: Is the US Stock Market Cheap Yet?
summary: The US stock market is currently trading at a 5% discount to its fair value, primarily due to significant selloffs in mega-cap technology and AI stocks. While valuations are more attractive than at the start of the year, they haven't reached the deep discounts seen in 2022. Morningstar strategists advise investors to consider overweighting value and small-cap stocks, and to wait for market stabilization before making major moves.

title: JEPI vs JEPQ: Which is a better covered call ETF to buy?
summary: This article compares two popular covered call ETFs from JPMorgan, JEPI and JEPQ, which are known for their high dividends. JEPI focuses on the S&P 500 while JEPQ targets the Nasdaq 100. The analysis concludes that JEPQ appears to be the better investment due to its higher dividend yield (11.23% vs. 7.80%) and superior total returns over the last three years (37% vs JEPI's unspecified lower return).

title: SMH ETF: The Growth Story Remains Incredibly Mispriced
summary: The article argues that the growth story in semiconductors, particularly AI-driven segments, remains undervalued despite a recovery in the Nasdaq. It highlights strong tech earnings, Nvidia's transition to Blackwell, and TSMC's investments as anchors for the AI thesis. The author suggests that current forward P/E and PEG ratios, along with resilient price action, indicate a buying opportunity for the SMH ETF.

title: REX Launches 3 New Growth & Income Covered Call ETFs
summary: REX Financial has introduced three new "Growth & Income Covered Call ETFs" (COII, MSII, TSII) targeting Coinbase, MicroStrategy, and Tesla, respectively. These ETFs aim to provide growth potential and weekly income through a dual portfolio strategy, writing call options on approximately half of the portfolio while maintaining uncapped upside potential on the remainder. This launch expands on their recently released NVIDIA-focused fund, catering to investor demand for income-generating products tied to high-growth companies.

title: Nvidia Stock Dips As Monolithic Power Crashes On Concerns of Supplier Allocation For Blackwell AI Chips: Retail Focuses On Earnings
summary: Nvidia's stock dropped following a report indicating that Monolithic Power might lose its role in supplying components for Nvidia's Blackwell processors due to performance issues. Shares of Monolithic Power crashed over 20%, while Renesas and Infineon are expected to take over the supply. Despite concerns, Deutsche Bank maintains a 'Buy' rating on Monolithic, and analysts remain optimistic about Nvidia's upcoming Q3 earnings report.

title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights NVIDIA, Amazon.com, Meta Platforms, Air T and Crown Crafts
summary: The Zacks Analyst Blog features research reports on 16 major stocks, including NVIDIA, Amazon.com, and Meta Platforms, along with micro-caps Air T and Crown Crafts. The blog provides insights into these companies' recent performance, growth drivers, and challenges, based on analysis by Zacks Equity Research. It also offers a daily "Ahead of Wall Street" article to prepare investors for market action.

title: Hedeker Wealth LLC Sells 1,574 Shares of Broadcom Inc. $AVGO
summary: Hedeker Wealth LLC reduced its stake in Broadcom Inc. by 1.6%, selling 1,574 shares, though Broadcom remains its second-largest holding at $27.18 million. Broadcom recently reported slightly better-than-expected earnings and has a significant market cap and P/E ratio, while institutional ownership stands at 76.4%. Various other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions in Broadcom, and several analysts have issued "Buy" or "Strong Buy" ratings for the semiconductor manufacturer.

title: Here Are Thursday's Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: AON, Apollo Global, Bullish, Jack Henry, Marsh & McClennan, Nasdaq, NVIDIA, and More
summary: This article compiles the top Wall Street analyst upgrades, downgrades, and initiations announced on Thursday, November 20, 2025, following NVIDIA's strong earnings report. It highlights key analyst calls for companies such as AON, Apollo Global Management, Bullish, Jack Henry, Marsh & McLennan, Nasdaq, and NVIDIA. The report also provides a market overview, including futures, Treasury bonds, oil and gas, gold, and cryptocurrency performance after NVIDIA's fiscal third-quarter results.

title: Blockfusion SPAC Merger: $450M Deal to List AI Data Center Company - News and Statistics
summary: Blockfusion, an AI data center company, is set to go public through a $450 million SPAC merger with Blue Acquisition Corp, with the combined entity expecting to trade on Nasdaq. This deal highlights the resurgence of SPACs and signals Blockfusion's transition into a high-performance, next-generation AI data center platform. The merger is expected to close in the first half of 2026, targeting $128 million in gross revenue and $75 million EBITDA by 2028.

title: FXAIX vs VOO: Is Fidelity or Vanguard Better for S&P 500 Exposure?
summary: This article compares Fidelity's FXAIX and Vanguard's VOO, both S&P 500 index funds, to help investors decide which is better for their needs. It analyzes their fees, structure (mutual fund vs. ETF), tax efficiency, performance, and suitability for different account types and trading styles. While FXAIX has a slightly lower expense ratio, VOO generally offers better tax efficiency and intraday tradability.

title: Trump Weighing Advanced Nvidia Chip Sales to China, Lutnick Says
summary: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick stated that President Donald Trump is currently considering whether to permit Nvidia Corp. to sell advanced artificial intelligence chips to China, with the final decision resting with him. Lutnick noted that Trump is consulting various advisers on the potential exports and has a deep understanding of Chinese President Xi Jinping. This comes after reports indicated US officials were already discussing the possibility of Nvidia selling its H200 AI chips to China.

title: Goldman Sachs revamps Broadcom stock price target with a twist
summary: Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider increased Broadcom's stock price target to $435, reiterating a buy rating, due to its critical role as an "arms dealer" in the AI boom. While expectations for Q4 are high, investors anticipate strong performance, significant AI sales, and a robust fiscal 2026 guide, particularly driven by its custom XPU business and collaborations with Google and OpenAI. Despite the stock's substantial run, the report highlights the challenge of meeting sky-high expectations.

title: YieldMax Short NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF To Go Ex-Dividend On November 28th, 2025 With 0.1241 USD Dividend Per Share
summary: The YieldMax Short NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (DIPS.US) is scheduled to go ex-dividend on November 28th, 2025, with a dividend of 0.1241 USD per share. Shareholders of record on that date will receive the dividend on December 1st, 2025. The article also provides brief explanations of cash and stock dividends.

title: Nvidia Set to Win US Approval to Export H200 AI Chips to China
summary: President Donald Trump has granted Nvidia Corp. permission to export its H200 artificial intelligence chips to China, a move that could help the company reclaim billions in lost revenue. This decision comes with a 25% surcharge and follows weeks of deliberations, with Trump announcing the agreement on Truth Social after informing Chinese President Xi Jinping. The exports will be limited to "approved customers," and other chipmakers like Intel and AMD will also be eligible.

title: ‘Greetings, earthlings’: Nvidia-backed Starcloud trains first AI model in space as orbital data center race heats up
summary: Nvidia-backed Starcloud has become the first to train an AI model from space, utilizing an Nvidia H100 GPU on its Starcloud-1 satellite to run Google's Gemma LLM. This achievement signals a significant step towards orbital data centers, offering a solution to the terrestrial data centers' energy demands and environmental impact. Starcloud plans to build a 5-gigawatt orbital data center powered by solar energy, with future launches incorporating Nvidia's Blackwell platform for enhanced AI performance.

title: Avista Corp director Widmann sells $285k in stock
summary: Avista Corp Director Janet D. Widmann sold 7,400 shares of common stock for $285,875 on December 12, 2025, reducing her direct ownership to 19,246 shares. This insider transaction follows Avista's strong Q3 2025 performance, where EPS exceeded expectations despite revenue slightly missing forecasts, leading Mizuho to raise its price target for Avista stock. InvestingPro offers detailed analysis of such insider activities and financial health for informed investment decisions regarding utility companies like Avista.

title: Is Morgan Stanley’s Backing Reframing Astera Labs (ALAB) as a Core AI Data Center Connectivity Play?
summary: Morgan Stanley recently highlighted Astera Labs (ALAB) as a key semiconductor pick for 2026, positioning it as a significant player in AI data center connectivity. The endorsement underscores Astera's role in hyperscale AI infrastructure and open-standards connectivity, particularly with its upcoming UA Link solutions.

title: Is Intuit (INTU) One of the Best Large Cap Stocks to Invest In?
summary: Intuit Inc. (NASDAQ: INTU) is highlighted as a top large-cap pick by BMO Capital Markets, expecting a strong tax season and potential growth from AI use cases in application and vertical software stocks. While recognizing INTU's potential, the article suggests other AI stocks may offer higher returns and lower downside risk. The financial technology and business software company is seen benefiting from increased M&A activity in "back office" software.

title: Is JAMA’s GUARDIAN Nod Quietly Reframing GeneDx (WGS) as a Core Newborn Genomics Platform?
summary: JAMA's recognition of GeneDx's GUARDIAN study highlights the potential of large-scale genomic newborn screening to detect treatable, often-missed childhood conditions and strengthens the clinical case for widespread adoption. While this bolsters GeneDx's investment narrative, the company's long-term success still hinges on broader payer and health system adoption, as well as managing reimbursement and pricing pressures. The FDA Breakthrough Device Designation also supports their efforts to build regulatory-grade evidence for future policy decisions and hospital integration.

title: Intel Stock (INTC): What to Know Before the Market Opens on Dec. 22, 2025
summary: This article provides a pre-market overview for Intel (INTC) stock on December 22, 2025, highlighting key catalysts such as Nvidia's cleared investment in Intel, the U.S. government's significant stake, and Intel's decision to retain its networking unit. It also discusses ongoing execution challenges, governance headlines, financial performance, and manufacturing roadmap updates that are influencing investor perception and analyst targets.

title: TSMC Arizona’s 3nm Acceleration: Bringing Advanced Manufacturing to US Soil
summary: TSMC has accelerated its roadmap for its Fab 21 complex in Phoenix, Arizona, with 3nm production now planned for 2027, a year ahead of schedule. The facility has achieved a 92% yield rate for 4nm chips and will soon begin the "tool-in" phase for 3nm production, significantly boosting U.S. domestic semiconductor manufacturing and safeguarding the AI hardware supply chain. This expansion, supported by the U.S. CHIPS Act, also includes advanced packaging capabilities and future plans for 2nm and 1.6nm nodes, solidifying America's role in global semiconductor production.

title: Wipro CTO Sandhya Arun outlines key technology trends set to shape enterprises in 2026
summary: Wipro CTO Sandhya Arun anticipates a significant shift in AI adoption by enterprises in 2026, moving from experimentation to widespread implementation across core business functions. Key trends include agentic AI networks managing workflows, embodied AI in physical systems, AI-enabled digital twins for simulation, and domain-native AI models. Talent readiness and continuous upskilling will be crucial for companies to leverage these advanced technologies effectively.

title: AMD Stock News and Forecasts for Dec. 26, 2025: China MI308 Signals, OpenAI Ramp, and Analyst Price Targets
summary: AMD stock is experiencing a "two-clock" dynamic at the close of 2025, balancing near-term regulatory hurdles for AI-chip approvals in China with longer-term growth driven by its 2026 rack-scale roadmap and partnerships with major customers like OpenAI and Oracle. Key catalysts include the potential for significant MI308 orders from China, institutional buying activity, and the anticipated deployments of MI450 GPUs starting in 2026. While analysts largely maintain a bullish stance with price targets suggesting notable upside, the stock's future performance hinges on both geopolitical stability regarding chip exports and the successful execution of its advanced AI infrastructure strategy.

title: Tech’s biggest bull lists his top 5 AI stocks for 2026, and Nvidia isn’t one of them
summary: Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives, a prominent technology bull, has released his top artificial-intelligence stock picks for 2026, notably excluding Nvidia NVDA. Ives highlighted the transformative potential of AI as a "fourth industrial revolution" while acknowledging investor concerns about the significant capital investment required. Despite the omission from his top five, Wedbush still considers Nvidia a leading technology stock for the upcoming year.

title: Baidu Stock Jumps on Plans To List AI Chip Unit Kunlunxin in Hong Kong
summary: Baidu's stock surged after its AI chip subsidiary, Kunlunxin, filed for an initial public offering on the Hong Kong stock exchange. Kunlunxin, which could be valued at up to $23 billion, is a significant part of Baidu's push into artificial intelligence. This move highlights Baidu's strategy to capitalize on its AI advancements through its specialized hardware unit.

title: Is It Time to Sell Your Tech Stocks and Reinvest Elsewhere?
summary: Morningstar Wealth strategists are recommending a shift from tech and AI stocks, which they consider overvalued after a strong run, toward more attractive opportunities. They have reduced exposure to US tech and communication services, reinvesting in areas like small-cap stocks and emerging markets, particularly Latin America, based on relative valuations and expected returns. The strategists see better value and higher expected returns in sectors such as healthcare compared to the current tech market.

title: Key facts: Nvidia signs $20B deal with Groq; stock rises on AI demand
summary: Nvidia has secured a $20 billion licensing deal with Groq for access to its inference processing units, strengthening Nvidia's focus on AI workloads. This deal and strong demand for data center hardware, with orders extending into 2027, contributed to an upward movement in Nvidia's stock. Investor enthusiasm for AI-related stocks has significantly boosted the company's market gains.

title: Hedeker Wealth LLC Has $9.13 Million Stock Holdings in ASML Holding N.V. $ASML
summary: Hedeker Wealth LLC significantly increased its ASML Holding N.V. (NASDAQ:ASML) stake by 99.7% in Q3, now holding 9,429 shares valued at $9.13 million, making it their 16th-largest position. The semiconductor company maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $1,171.83, supported by strong fundamentals and positive analyst sentiment driven by AI-related demand for its EUV technology. ASML also beat quarterly EPS estimates, boasts a 27% net margin, and has raised its quarterly dividend.

title: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) Is Down 11.0% After Surging Mission‑Critical AI Deals - Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) has seen its stock price drop by 11.0% despite a surge in new mission-critical AI contracts across government and commercial sectors. The article examines whether this recent share price weakness, set against the backdrop of expanding partnerships and significant deal flow, changes the investment narrative for the leading AI software provider. While the new deals support near-term growth, the stock's rich valuation and volatility continue to be points of contention among investors, leading to a wide range of fair value estimates.

title: Buy this mega-cap AI chip stock ahead of this catalyst next week: Morgan Stanley
summary: Morgan Stanley is recommending investors buy TSMC (TSM) stock ahead of its Q4 2025 analyst meeting on January 15, which could act as a catalyst. The firm believes TSMC could see its share price rise if it guides 25% year-over-year revenue growth for 2026 and raises its AI semiconductor foundry revenue CAGR for 2024-2029 to 60%. Morgan Stanley outlines bullish, base, and weaker scenarios for the stock based on TSMC's guidance.

title: Is Nvidia disrupting HVAC? Johnson Controls, Trane shares dip amid cooling shift
summary: Shares of HVAC companies like Johnson Controls and Trane Technologies dipped after Nvidia's CEO stated that their new Rubin chip platform would require less traditional cooling, impacting future data center HVAC needs. This shift could benefit companies specializing in liquid cooling solutions like nVent Electric and Vertiv Holdings, while exposing traditional air cooling providers to potential headwinds.

title: Better Broad-Market ETF: Schwab's SCHB vs. iShares' ITOT
summary: This article compares two broad-market ETFs, Schwab's SCHB and iShares' ITOT, highlighting their similarities in cost, performance, and holdings, as both aim to offer broad exposure to the total U.S. stock market. While nearly identical in investment approach, ITOT is noted for its larger assets under management and slightly higher liquidity compared to SCHB. The choice between them ultimately comes down to investor preference for the fund manager.

title: Ninety One UK Ltd Sells 72,787 Shares of Alphabet Inc. $GOOG
summary: Ninety One UK Ltd significantly reduced its stake in Alphabet Inc. by selling 72,787 shares, leaving it with 119,337 shares valued at $29.07 million. Despite this, analysts largely maintain a "Buy" rating for Alphabet, with an average price target of $314.64, driven by growth in Google Cloud, Gemini AI, and strategic distribution wins. Noteworthy insider selling, including by CEO Sundar Pichai, has also occurred, with insiders offloading 195,465 shares in the last 90 days.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Says Chinese Approval For H200 AI Chips Will Show Up Quietly In Purchase Orders: 'Not Expecting Any Press Releases' - NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA), Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM)
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated that Chinese government approval for importing the H200 AI chips will be seen through customer purchase orders rather than any official public announcement. This comes as Nvidia awaits U.S. government export licenses for advanced chips to China, with the company forecasting a "really giant year" for demand. Despite regulatory hurdles, interest from Chinese firms remains strong, and Nvidia showcased new chips for its Vera Rubin platform at CES 2026.

title: A Look At DTE Energy (DTE) Valuation After Major 1.4 GW Data Center Agreement
summary: DTE Energy (DTE) shares are gaining attention after the company secured a significant 1.4 GW agreement to supply power to a hyperscale data center, with potential for an additional 3 GW. Despite recent short-term share price dips, the stock has shown strong long-term performance. Analysts currently view DTE as undervalued, with a fair value of $149.15 against its current trading price of $128.26, though execution and regulatory risks remain.

title: Jim Cramer Calls Nvidia, Eli Lilly Partnership 'Monumental Effort' To 'Speed Up' Critical Drug Creation
summary: Jim Cramer highlighted the underappreciated synergy between Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) and Eli Lilly And Co. <a class="ticker-link" data-ticker="LLY" data-exchange="NYSE" href="https://www.benzinga.com/quote/LLY" target="_blank" rel="noop

title: BofA Touts Uber Technologies Inc. (UBER) Prospects in Autonomous Vehicles on Nvidia Partnership
summary: BofA Securities has reiterated a Buy rating on Uber Technologies Inc. (UBER) with a $119 price target, citing the company's strong positioning in the autonomous vehicle market through a strategic partnership with Nvidia. This collaboration is expected to accelerate Level 4 autonomous technology adoption by lowering entry barriers for OEMs, thus benefiting Uber's network with more autonomous vehicles. Bernstein SocGen analysts also maintain an Outperform rating and a $115 price target on Uber stock, despite increasing competition in the AV market.

title: VanEck Semiconductor ETF Gains as AI Leaders Surge
summary: The VanEck Semiconductor ETF ($SMH) has seen a 2.49% rise and a significant net inflow of $884.21 million over the past week, driven by strong market sentiment surrounding its top AI-related holdings. Key contributors include Nvidia (NVDA), which continues to lead the AI boom with substantial projected upside, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), which is reinforcing its position as a critical manufacturer for AI chips. Broadcom (AVGO) is also emerging as a preferred AI beneficiary due to its custom accelerators and networking chips.

title: azz stock hits all-time high at 123.32 USD
summary: AZZ Inc. stock has reached an all-time high of $123.32, marking a 42.37% increase over the past year. Despite trading near its 52-week high, analysis suggests the stock might still be undervalued, supported by a strong financial health score and analyst price targets up to $141. This performance is bolstered by impressive Q3 2026 earnings, which exceeded both EPS and revenue expectations.

title: Richard Hayne Sells 10,667 Shares of Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN) Stock
summary: Richard Hayne, CEO of Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN), recently sold 10,667 shares of the company's stock for approximately $748,823, reducing his stake by 0.06%. The transaction, dated January 16th, occurred at an average price of $70.20 per share, with Hayne still owning over 17.6 million shares valued at about $1.239 billion. Analysts currently maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Urban Outfitters, with an average price target of $86.67.

title: Why AMD Stock Jumped Today
summary: Shares of Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) climbed over 7% today after positive commentary from Wall Street analysts regarding the chipmaker's prospects. Analysts cited robust demand for AMD's data center CPUs, with one suggesting the company may already be nearly sold out of server CPUs for 2026 and could raise prices. Demand for AI-related sales and strong performance in the server business are also contributing to the optimistic outlook.

title: Teradyne (TER) Deep Dive: The Gatekeeper of the AI Hardware Boom
summary: Teradyne (TER) has transformed into a critical player in the AI hardware ecosystem, moving from a cyclical automated test equipment provider to an indispensable gatekeeper for high-performance computing and AI infrastructure. The company's strategic pivot towards AI-driven robotics and high-bandwidth memory testing, along with its strong financial performance and leadership, positions it for continued secular growth despite valuation and geopolitical risks. Investors are watching its upcoming Q4 earnings and the ramp-up of its Michigan robotics hub as key indicators of future performance.

title: Jim Cramer on Ares Capital: "I Want to Be Very Careful There"
summary: Jim Cramer expressed caution regarding Ares Capital (ARCC), stating he wants to "be very careful there" due to uncertainty about its holdings and a high 9% yield that makes him nervous. He suggested switching out of ARCC and into Johnson & Johnson (J&J). Cramer discussed other holdings like Apple, Hershey, Kimberly-Clark, and NVIDIA, and suggested that while ARCC provides financing solutions to middle-market companies, other AI stocks might offer better upside potential with less risk.

title: Why DRDGOLD (DRD) Is Up 5.2% After Strong Earnings Momentum And Technical Breakout - And What's Next
summary: DRDGOLD (DRD) has seen a 5.2% increase in its stock price due to strong earnings momentum, high return on equity, minimal debt, and technical strength, positioning it as a potential CAN SLIM candidate. The company's recent performance is reinforced by its shares trading near a 52-week high, although operational risks and commodity price sensitivity remain key considerations for investors. Different fair value estimates highlight varied investor expectations regarding the stock's upside potential and risks.

title: Nvidia (NVDA) extends AI beyond data centers with new Hesai (HSAI) partnership
summary: The article reports on Nvidia's partnership with Hesai, a leading developer of LiDAR solutions, to expand AI applications beyond data centers. This collaboration marks Nvidia's entry into the burgeoning field of AI-powered autonomous systems and highlights their strategy to integrate advanced AI capabilities across various industries, utilizing Hesai's expertise in perception technology. This partnership signifies Nvidia's continued diversification and commitment to advancing real-world AI implementations.

title: Should Viatris’ (VTRS) First Inpefa UAE Launch Shape Investor Views on Its Innovation Strategy?
summary: Viatris recently launched Inpefa (sotagliflozin) in the UAE, marking its first international commercialization of this heart failure treatment outside the U.S. and Europe, stemming from a licensing deal with Lexicon Pharmaceuticals. This move highlights Viatris's strategy to expand its innovative, branded therapy portfolio and broadens patient access, although its immediate impact on the company's substantial revenue base is expected to be modest. Investors should consider this as one of several catalysts, alongside cost discipline and debt reduction, as Viatris continues its multi-year transformation while managing ongoing net losses and dividend coverage.

title: Should TCW Transform 500 ETF (VOTE) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: The TCW Transform 500 ETF (VOTE), launched in 2021, offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Blend segment of the US equity market with low annual operating expenses of 0.05%. The ETF holds a Zacks ETF Rank of 2 (Buy) and has performed well, returning 1.47% year-to-date and 14.95% over the last year, making it an attractive option for investors. Its top holdings include Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, with a significant allocation to the Information Technology sector.

title: Unitil increases quarterly dividend by 5.5 percent
summary: Unitil Corporation announced a 5.5% increase in its quarterly common stock dividend to $0.475 per share, marking its 11th consecutive year of dividend increases. The annualized dividend is now $1.90 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 12, 2026. Despite the dividend hike and a recent revenue beat in Q3 2025, InvestingPro's Fair Value assessment indicates the company, a public utility operating in New England, appears overvalued with a current dividend yield of 3.6% and a P/E ratio of 17.36.

title: Tenaris SA ADR stock hits 52-week high at 45.45 USD
summary: Tenaris SA ADR stock has reached a 52-week high of $45.45, marking a 23.48% appreciation over the past year. The company demonstrates low price volatility with a beta of 0.56 and possesses a strong financial position with more cash than debt and a P/E ratio of 12.19. Analysts, including Bernstein's Guillaume Delaby, maintain an Outperform rating, citing a strong outlook for the oil services industry, increased offshore backlogs, and potential rises in upstream spending.

title: Arm Holdings Deepens AI Ties With OpenAI And Meta Data Centers
summary: Arm Holdings is expanding its role in the AI and data center markets by developing an AI XPI ASIC for OpenAI and a custom server CPU potentially for Meta. These projects signify Arm's push beyond traditional smartphone chip architectures into high-performance AI silicon and data center computing. The deeper involvement in these areas could broaden Arm's addressable market and solidify its position amidst hyperscale clients, though it also introduces increased execution complexity and competition.

title: Explaining Taiwan’s lead in advanced semiconductor manufacturing
summary: Semiconductor industry veteran Marco Mezger explains Taiwan's continued dominance in advanced semiconductor manufacturing, attributing it to decades of sustained investment in manufacturing processes, equipment, and engineering know-how, rather than a single breakthrough. He notes that China faces significant hurdles in replicating this complex ecosystem, focusing on design, manufacturing, and domestic supply chains simultaneously. Mezger also highlights Taiwan's focus on foundry operations, which has allowed for specialization in yield, reliability, and process optimization.

title: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods Lift Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc. (ESQ) Target After In-Line Core Performance
summary: Keefe, Bruyette & Woods has raised its price target for Esquire Financial Holdings, Inc. (ESQ) to $85 from $80, maintaining a Market Perform rating, following the company's core net interest income aligning with expectations. Esquire Financial reported net interest income of $33.3 million, a net interest margin of 6.05%, and an increase in book value per share to $33.86. The company, a digital-first commercial bank, continues to focus on the legal industry and small businesses with tech-enabled financial solutions.

title: Summa Corp. Buys 9,827 Shares of NVIDIA Corporation $NVDA
summary: Summa Corp. increased its stake in NVIDIA Corporation by 1.6%, purchasing an additional 9,827 shares, making NVIDIA its largest holding at 50.4% of its portfolio. Despite significant insider selling over the past 90 days, analysts remain largely bullish on NVIDIA with an average target price of $263.98, driven by positive catalysts like China's conditional approval for chip sales and potential investment in OpenAI. The article also details other institutional investor activity, recent insider transactions, analyst rating changes, and key news impacting NVIDIA's stock.

title: Oracle stock in flux following $50 billion AI fundraising announcement
summary: Oracle plans to raise $45 billion to $50 billion this year to fund its expanding artificial intelligence infrastructure. This announcement caused significant fluctuations in the company's stock, which had previously dropped over 50% from its peak last year, though it saw a brief recovery before dipping again. The capital influx aims to meet demand from major clients like AMD, Meta, Nvidia, and OpenAI, and will be financed through a combination of debt and equity issuances.

title: Envirotech Vehicles’ Shares Skyrocket Following AI Advancements
summary: Envirotech Vehicles' stock surged by 9.96% after announcing an order for Nvidia GPUs expected to generate $107M in revenue and the acquisition of AZIO AI to bolster its AI infrastructure. Despite historical losses, the company's aggressive push into AI and strategic mergers are boosting investor confidence and positioning it for future growth in AI and mobility solutions. These strategic moves aim to enhance market standing and provide flexible capital opportunities.

title: Taiwan's ASE sees its advanced packaging business doubling to $3.2 billion in 2026
summary: ASE Technology Holding expects its advanced packaging business to double to $3.2 billion in 2026. This projection follows a strong fourth-quarter revenue report and significant planned capital expenditures for machinery and facilities to support growth in the sector, particularly as its subsidiary SPIL is a key supplier for Nvidia's AI chips.

title: Regency Centers earnings beat by $0.55, revenue fell short of estimates
summary: Regency Centers (NASDAQ: REG) reported Q4 EPS of $1.12, surpassing analyst estimates of $0.57 by $0.55. However, revenue for the quarter came in at $395.41M, falling short of the consensus estimate of $404.31M. The company's stock has shown positive performance over the last 3 and 12 months, and InvestingPro rates its financial health as "great performance".

title: Navigating the iShares MSCI World ETF: Key Dates and Shifting Sentiment
summary: Investor sentiment around the iShares MSCI World ETF (URTH) is becoming more cautious due to an upcoming MSCI index review and a broader market rotation away from risk-sensitive assets. The ETF, with over 70% in US equities and significant tech concentration, has experienced recent outflows as investors make tactical adjustments before the February 10, 2026 rebalancing and a more substantial methodology overhaul in May 2026. This "risk-off" mood and commodity shift could lead to short-term consolidation for the fund.

title: 5 Essential Analyst Inquiries From Kforce’s Fourth Quarter Earnings Call
summary: Kforce (KFRC) experienced a stock price downturn after its Q4 2025 earnings due to lower revenue and missed EPS expectations, attributed to softness in the tech services industry. Despite challenges, management expressed optimism for sequential gains in technology and flexible staffing, outlining strategies for increasing billing rates and driving margin improvement in 2026. Analysts focused on the durability of improvements, AI-related demand, budget reallocations, and future revenue growth, with the company looking to consulting-led growth and cost controls.

title: Iambic could earn almost $2 billion in new Takeda deal
summary: Iambic Therapeutics, an AI and physics-based drug discovery startup, has secured a deal with Takeda Pharmaceutical that could be worth nearly $2 billion. Takeda will utilize Iambic's NeuralPLexer AI platform for small-molecule drug discovery in oncology, inflammation, and gastrointestinal diseases. This follows a recent $100 million funding round for Iambic, which claims its combined AI and physics approach offers more accurate and data-efficient drug discovery.

title: TSMC Stock Is on a Roll. Rising Sales and Trump Tariff News Could Keep It Going.
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) reported a 20% increase in January revenue from the previous quarter, signaling its strong position in the semiconductor industry. This growth, coupled with potential U.S. tariff relief, could further boost the company's performance. The article suggests that TSMC's stock momentum is likely to continue.

title: 8,973 Shares in Moody's Corporation $MCO Bought by Katamaran Capital LLP
summary: Katamaran Capital LLP initiated a new position in Moody's Corporation (NYSE:MCO), acquiring 8,973 shares valued at approximately $4.275 million, making it their 12th largest holding. Institutional investors collectively own 92.11% of Moody's. The stock has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $558.56.

title: Oklo (NYSE:OKLO) Stock Price Down 7.4% - Time to Sell?
summary: Oklo Inc. (NYSE:OKLO) experienced a 7.4% stock price drop on Tuesday, trading at $69.46, significantly below its moving averages, with lower-than-average trading volume. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus but have mixed ratings and a target price of $103.20, while the company missed Q3 EPS estimates and insiders have recently sold substantial shares. These factors suggest potential concerns for investors.

title: Owning Bitcoin Through an ETF: Scale or Lower Fees With IBIT and HODL
summary: This article compares two Bitcoin ETFs, iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) and VanEck Bitcoin ETF (HODL), highlighting their differences in expense ratios, assets under management (AUM), and liquidity. While HODL offers a lower expense ratio, IBIT boasts significantly larger AUM and trading volume, potentially offering better liquidity. The choice between them depends on an investor's strategy, with IBIT favoring those who trade frequently and HODL suiting long-term buy-and-hold investors.

title: 2 Software Stocks with Promising Prospects and 1 We Question
summary: This article evaluates three software stocks, recommending two for their promising prospects and questioning one. Asure Software is advised against due to slow revenue growth, unchanged operating margins, and weak free cash flow. In contrast, Shopify and Braze are highlighted as strong contenders, citing their impressive revenue growth, market share gains, and healthy pipelines for future expansion.

title: Pilgrim's Pride's Q4 Earnings Call: Our Top 5 Analyst Questions
summary: Pilgrim's Pride's Q4 results showed revenue growth but missed adjusted EPS expectations due to margin compression, despite strong demand in U.S. retail and foodservice. CEO Fabio Sandri cited persistent inflation and commodity market pressure, particularly in Mexico and Europe, as key factors affecting profitability. Analysts' questions focused on supply-side pressures, commodity price sustainability, Mexico's market volatility, foodservice dynamics, and the impact of diseases in Europe.

title: Leerink cuts Gossamer Bio stock rating on trial miss

Wainwright have reiterated their positive ratings due to belief in the company's broader pipeline and potential.

title: Beamr Issues Annual CEO Letter to Shareholders: From Validation to Execution in AI Video
summary: Beamr Imaging Ltd. (NASDAQ: BMR) issued its annual Letter to Shareholders from CEO Sharon Carmel, highlighting significant achievements in 2025, particularly the validation of its AI video technology for the autonomous vehicle (AV) sector. The letter outlines Beamr's strategy for 2026, focusing on further developing AI video solutions for both human and machine vision, and converting its commercial pipeline into tangible outcomes. Despite a slight revenue increase, the company reported a net loss of $6 million in 2025 due to increased R&D and sales and marketing expenses, but maintains a solid balance sheet.

title: Caterpillar Inc Stock Moved Down by 3.82% on Mar 3: What Signal Does It Send?
summary: Caterpillar Inc (CAT) stock declined by 3.82% on March 3, 2026, primarily due to cautious analyst outlooks, particularly GuruFocus's assessment of significant overvaluation, and the financial burden of anticipated tariffs. The company faces an estimated $2.6 billion in tariff costs for 2026, putting pressure on profit margins despite strong Q4 2025 earnings. While CAT has strong fundamentals and outperformed the S&P 500 Industrials sector over the past year, concerns about ongoing tariff impacts and legal proceedings remain.

title: Onto Innovation : ONTO
summary: Onto Innovation Inc. (ONTO) is a technology company specializing in advanced process control tools for semiconductor manufacturing, including optical metrology, lithography systems, and analytical software. The company, founded in 1940 and based in Wilmington, Massachusetts, serves various high-tech industries. An article from "The AI Investor Podcast" suggests that Onto Innovation should be considered a strong semiconductor play in 2026.

title: 3 Reasons CALY is Risky and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: This article analyzes Callaway Golf Company (CALY) and identifies three reasons why it is considered a risky investment despite recent stock price increases. The concerns include declining constant currency revenue, a projected decrease in cash flow margin, and a consistent decline in Return on Invested Capital (ROIC). The author suggests investors consider alternative opportunities, particularly in digital advertising and momentum stocks, as CALY's current valuation seems to price in too much good news.

title: Is Digital Realty Trust Stock Outperforming the Dow?
summary: Digital Realty Trust (DLR), a large-cap REIT specializing in data centers, has significantly outperformed the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) over the past three months, year-to-date, and over the past 52 weeks. The company's focus on AI and high-density computing, along with strong Q4 results, has contributed to its bullish trend. Despite lagging behind close rival Equinix (EQIX) in some short-term metrics, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an optimistic price target.

title: SMCI Revenue Jumped 123% Year-Over-Year: Here’s the 85% Upside Scenario
summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) reported a 123% year-over-year revenue increase in Q2 FY2026, reaching $12.7 billion, and raised its full-year revenue floor to $40 billion, yet its stock trades nearly 48% below its 52-week high due to margin compression. The company's strategy for margin recovery hinges on its Data Center Building Block Solutions (DCBBS) and new higher-margin product lines. Despite a cautious Wall Street, TIKR's valuation model suggests an 84.9% upside, contingent on the success of these margin improvement initiatives.

title: 2 Reasons SF is Risky and 1 Stock to Buy Instead

The valuation of Stifel is considered reasonable, but the company doesn't pass the "quality test" of the analysts.

title: Cosmos Health purchases $600,000 in Bitcoin for treasury
summary: Cosmos Health Inc. has added another $600,000 in Bitcoin to its treasury, bringing its total digital asset holdings in Bitcoin and Ethereum to $3.1 million. This cryptocurrency allocation constitutes approximately 25% of the company's $12.65 million market capitalization, reflecting a strategy to diversify its balance sheet despite its stock performance and debt burden. CEO Greg Siokas stated that digital assets are viewed as having strong upside potential and could potentially be monetized to address the company's perceived undervaluation.

title: QUALCOMM, Inc. (QCOM) Partners with Keysight to Advance 5G emerging 6G networks
summary: Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) has partnered with Keysight to advance 5G and emerging 6G networks by developing high-precision radio-frequency digital twins. This collaboration aims to optimize Multiple-Input Multiple-Output (MIMO) systems and demonstrate how digital twins can bridge the gap between simulation and real-world network performance. Initial tests have shown promising results for scalable advanced MIMO configurations and AI-native physical-layer enhancements for 6G wireless systems.

title: Black Swift Group LLC Cuts Position in NVIDIA Corporation $NVDA
summary: Black Swift Group LLC significantly reduced its stake in NVIDIA Corporation by 91.5% in Q3 2025, selling 35,094 shares and retaining 3,250 shares. Despite this and other insider selling totaling over $229.9 million, NVIDIA reported strong Q4 results with a 73.2% year-over-year revenue increase. Analysts maintain a consensus "Buy" rating for NVDA with an average target price of $274.21.

title: STMicroelectronics accelerates global adoption and market growth of Physical AI with NVIDIA
summary: STMicroelectronics is partnering with NVIDIA to accelerate the development and adoption of Physical AI systems like humanoid robots. This collaboration involves integrating ST sensors, microcontrollers, and motor control solutions with the NVIDIA robotics ecosystem, including the Holoscan Sensor Bridge and Isaac Sim. The initiative aims to streamline the design, training, and deployment of physical AI systems by simplifying hardware integration and improving simulation accuracy.

title: Skyworks Solutions Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
summary: Skyworks Solutions Inc. (SWKS) stock fell on Wednesday, underperforming its competitors. The stock closed at $99.98, marking a 0.28% decrease, while the S&P 500 also saw a slight decline. The company's performance has been volatile recently, with mixed results compared to weekly and 52-week highs and lows.

title: Navitas Debuts Revolutionary 800 V-6 V Power Delivery Board at NVIDIA GTC 2026
summary: Navitas Semiconductor has unveiled a new 800 V–6 V DC-DC power delivery board at NVIDIA GTC 2026, designed to eliminate the traditional 48 V intermediate bus converter stage in compute server trays. This innovation aims to enhance system efficiency, reliability, and compute density by enabling direct conversion from 800 V to 6 V. The solution targets high-power AI infrastructure, supporting the transition to 800 VDC data center power and improving overall system performance and power density.

title: Should Super Micro Computer’s Expanded NVIDIA AI Factory Lineup at GTC 2026 Require Action From SMCI Investors?
summary: Super Micro Computer recently expanded its NVIDIA-aligned AI infrastructure portfolio at GTC 2026, introducing new systems based on Blackwell and Vera Rubin platforms, and a context memory (CMX) storage server. These additions solidify Supermicro's position as a comprehensive "AI factory" partner, offering full-stack solutions. While these advancements reinforce the near-term AI growth story, investors should also consider risks like margin compression, customer concentration, and potential hardware commoditization.
